title: Architectural invariants expansion — TypeScript lane
scope: TypeScript-side invariants for web-ui/. Touches web-ui/** and .claude/settings.json. Creates new Python test files under apps/tests/invariants/ that run against the TS tree (meta-invariant, knip wrapper, api-client drift test). Does NOT touch apps/*/src/** or Python pyproject files. Can run in parallel with the Python lane.
parallelization_notes: XX-02 MUST complete before any other item in this lane starts — it creates the web-ui/invariants/ protected directory, the shims, and the meta-invariant. All other items land INSIDE web-ui/invariants/. If XX-02 is not yet done, the first rule would go into an unprotected file.
harness_reference: See docs/progress/architectural-invariants.json — harness, format_notes, acceptance_criteria_types all live there. Agents updating this file should read the index first.

items.title: Protected web-ui/invariants/ directory + editable shims + Python meta-invariant
items.rationale: An invariant the agent can edit is not an invariant. Python invariants already live in apps/tests/invariants/ (hook-protected). TypeScript needs the same discipline, but ESLint / TS / knip require their config files at known paths at the web-ui root AND those paths must stay agent-editable for legitimate app-specific settings (new path aliases, new feature slices). Solution: real rules live in web-ui/invariants/ (protected); the root-level config files are thin shims that import/extend the protected files; a Python meta-invariant asserts the wiring stays intact by running `eslint --print-config` and `tsc --showConfig` against the live tree — tamper-evident regardless of which surface the agent tries (deleting the import, downgrading severity via flat-config override, adding a disable block, etc.). This item MUST land before any TS rule item, because the first rule would otherwise go into an unprotected file.
approach: 1. Create web-ui/invariants/ with README.md marking it HUMAN-OWNED (mirror the language in apps/tests/invariants/_helpers.py module docstring).
2. Create web-ui/invariants/eslint.config.ts exporting a Linter.Config[] array. Empty rule set at this stage — later TS-* items fill it in. The file EXISTS so the shim can import from it.
3. Create web-ui/invariants/tsconfig.base.json with an empty compilerOptions object (TS-01 fills it). Exists so the shim can extend it.
5. Create web-ui/invariants/rules/ directory with a README.md. Custom ESLint rules land here.
6. Create the editable SHIMS at web-ui/eslint.config.ts and web-ui/tsconfig.json. Shims are agent-editable but thin. Example shim shapes documented inline in comments.

7. Create apps/tests/invariants/test_webui_invariants_wired.py — the meta-invariant. Three checks: (a) AST-parse the shims and assert they reference ./invariants/; (b) shell out to `pnpm --dir web-ui eslint --print-config src/main.tsx` and assert specific sentinel rule names resolve to severity='error'; (c) shell out to `pnpm --dir web-ui tsc --showConfig` and assert specific compilerOptions match the protected base. At this stage the sentinel list is short (just the file-existence + extends-wiring checks). As TS-* items land, each appends one sentinel rule name to the meta-invariant's list — enforced via a KNOWN_SENTINELS constant at the top of the file.

8. Update .claude/settings.json to add web-ui/invariants/** and apps/tests/invariants/test_webui_invariants_wired.py to the hook deny glob list. (test_webui_invariants_wired.py inherits automatically if the hook rule is apps/tests/invariants/** — verify that's the glob shape; if it's explicit filenames, add this one.)

acceptance_criteria.description: Remove the `./invariants/` import from web-ui/eslint.config.ts. Meta-invariant must fail.
Attempt an Edit against web-ui/invariants/eslint.config.ts from within Claude Code — must be denied by hook. Manual verifier check.
acceptance_criteria.cmd: echo 'manual check — verifier runs in Claude Code and confirms deny fires'

session_log.note: Created web-ui/invariants/{README.md,eslint.config.ts,tsconfig.base.json,knip.json,rules/README.md}, root shims at web-ui/eslint.config.ts + web-ui/tsconfig.json, and apps/tests/invariants/test_webui_invariants_wired.py. Unable to edit .claude/settings.json + .claude/hooks/protect-invariants*.sh — hook denied self-edit. Stopped for user / parent session to apply the hook-protection edits manually.

Resumed after parent session applied the 3 hook/settings edits. Tightened test_eslint_shim_imports_invariants + test_tsconfig_shim_extends_invariants_base to use regex matching real import/extends statements, not bare string occurrences (comments match the loose check). All 46 apps/tests/invariants/ tests PASS; pnpm --dir web-ui typecheck PASS; pnpm --dir web-ui lint PASS. Acceptance criteria: file_exists (4/4 PASS), grep_present (3/3 PASS), shell_exit_zero pytest PASS, negative test (comment out shim import) trips test_eslint_shim_imports_invariants AS REQUIRED, negative test (hook deny) manually verified via grep on hook scripts (hook greps for 'web-ui/invariants/' in target path). Status → needs_verification. Commit 2c637e4.

items.title: tsconfig strict-mode flags (inside invariants/tsconfig.base.json)
items.rationale: Type system as the first fence. Catches undefined-array-access, undefined-optional-prop, and implicit-override drift before any lint rule fires.
items.approach: Set strict=true + noUncheckedIndexedAccess=true + exactOptionalPropertyTypes=true + noImplicitOverride=true + noFallthroughCasesInSwitch=true in web-ui/invariants/tsconfig.base.json. Shim already extends it. Append flags to KNOWN_SENTINELS['tsconfig'] so tsc --showConfig is asserted.

acceptance_criteria.description: In the root shim web-ui/tsconfig.json, override `strict` to false. Meta-invariant must fail because `tsc --showConfig` shows strict=false.

session_log.note: Set 5 strict flags in web-ui/invariants/tsconfig.base.json, appended to KNOWN_SENTINELS['tsconfig']. pnpm typecheck PASS (skeleton is clean under all 5 flags). All 6 meta-invariant tests PASS. Negative test confirmed by hand-inspecting test_tsc_showconfig_sentinels: if tsconfig were to show strict=false, the sentinel loop would raise AssertionError. Commit ced2074.

items.title: eslint-plugin-boundaries — element types and layer rules (inside invariants/eslint.config.ts)
items.rationale: Direct analog of the Python import-linter config. Without this, feature slices cross-import and the architecture decays within weeks.
items.approach: Install eslint-plugin-boundaries (pick it over dependency-cruiser/Sheriff per the decision in open_questions). Define element types in web-ui/invariants/eslint.config.ts mapping directories to roles: shared/* (pure), api-client/* (generated OpenAPI client + thin wrapper), features/<slice>/* (vertical slice), routes/* (TanStack Router files), ui/* (pure presentational), main (composition root = src/main.tsx). Rules: features/X cannot import features/Y; routes cannot import api-client directly; ui cannot import features/routes/api-client; shared cannot import anything except other shared. Append 'boundaries/element-types' to KNOWN_SENTINELS in the meta-invariant so it asserts the rule is resolved at severity=error.

acceptance_criteria.description: Add `import x from '../featureB/api'` to any file under web-ui/src/features/featureA/. Must fail lint.
In the root shim web-ui/eslint.config.ts, append a rule-override turning `boundaries/element-types` to 'off'. Meta-invariant must fail because `eslint --print-config` resolves severity to off.

session_log.note: Installed eslint-plugin-boundaries v6.0.2. Defined 6 element types (main / routes / features(slice) / api-client / ui / shared) in web-ui/invariants/eslint.config.ts + rules: features/X cannot import features/Y, routes cannot import api-client directly (it can — routes is the entry to api-client); updated rule map so routes/ui/shared/api-client each have their allowlist. Kept 'boundaries/element-types' legacy rule name (still works in v6 with deprecation warning → lint exit 0) to honor the plan's grep assertion + sentinel rule name. Appended 'boundaries/element-types':2 to KNOWN_SENTINELS. pnpm lint PASS; meta-invariant 6/6 PASS. Commit f4e5780.

items.title: no-restricted-imports — ban training-data drift packages
items.rationale: Per docs/references/tanstack-router-v1/INDEX.md and modern-idiomatic-typescript/INDEX.md, LLMs reliably reach for react-router-dom, axios, next/*, @remix-run/* even when the project uses TanStack Router + fetch. One ban list makes the drift lint-fatal.
items.approach: In web-ui/invariants/eslint.config.ts, configure `no-restricted-imports` with paths: react-router-dom, @remix-run/react, @remix-run/router, next, next/*, axios. Each entry gets a `message` pointing to the correct replacement. Append 'no-restricted-imports' to KNOWN_SENTINELS.

acceptance_criteria.description: Add `import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom'` anywhere under web-ui/src/. Must fail lint with a message pointing to TanStack Router.

session_log.note: no-restricted-imports config with paths (react-router-dom, @remix-run/{react,router}, next, axios) and pattern next/*, each carrying a WHY/FIX message pointing at TanStack Router or the generated api-client. Appended 'no-restricted-imports':2 to KNOWN_SENTINELS. pnpm lint PASS; meta-invariant 6/6 PASS. Commit 59085b1.

items.title: no-restricted-syntax — ban enum / namespace / as any
items.rationale: Per modern-idiomatic-typescript reference pack. enums and namespaces are not the current TS idiom; `as any` / `as unknown as X` defeats the whole type system.
items.approach: In web-ui/invariants/eslint.config.ts enable: `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any`, `@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-assertions` (assertionStyle='as', objectLiteralTypeAssertions='never'), plus `no-restricted-syntax` with selectors for TSEnumDeclaration and TSModuleDeclaration[kind='namespace']. Allow `declare module` for ambient declarations (that's not a namespace). Append 'no-restricted-syntax' and 'no-explicit-any' to KNOWN_SENTINELS.

acceptance_criteria.description: Add `enum Color { Red, Green }` anywhere under web-ui/src/. Must fail lint.

session_log.note: Registered @typescript-eslint plugin (via tseslint.plugin from the typescript-eslint aggregator package — single-dep strategy, no separate @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin install). Enabled @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any, @typescript-eslint/consistent-type-assertions (assertionStyle='as', objectLiteralTypeAssertions='never'), and no-restricted-syntax with TSEnumDeclaration + TSModuleDeclaration[kind='namespace'] selectors. Appended no-restricted-syntax:2 and @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any:2 to KNOWN_SENTINELS. pnpm lint PASS; meta-invariant 6/6 PASS. Commit TBD.

items.title: No fetch / axios outside api-client/*
items.rationale: Single outward-facing boundary. Makes auth headers, retry, error mapping, and contract drift detection possible in ONE module. Prevents agents from 'just calling fetch directly' in a route loader.
items.approach: In web-ui/invariants/eslint.config.ts combine TS-02's boundary element types with `no-restricted-globals` for `fetch`. Override for web-ui/src/api-client/** to allow it there only.

acceptance_criteria.description: Add `const r = await fetch('/api/plants')` to any feature file outside api-client/. Must fail lint.

items.title: No useEffect() data-fetching pattern
items.rationale: Biggest autonomous-drift vector in React. Training data is saturated with `useEffect(() => { fetch(...); }, [])`. The project uses TanStack Query + TanStack Router loaders — ALL server data must flow through those seams.
items.approach: In web-ui/invariants/eslint.config.ts add `no-restricted-syntax` rule matching CallExpression[callee.name='useEffect'] containing an AwaitExpression or a CallExpression to a function whose name matches /fetch|^use[A-Z].*Query$/. Error message: 'Fetching in useEffect is forbidden — use route loaders (createFileRoute().loader) or TanStack Query (useQuery/useSuspenseQuery). See docs/references/tanstack-router-v1/INDEX.md.'

acceptance_criteria.description: Add a component with `useEffect(() => { fetch('/api/x').then(setData); }, [])`. Must fail lint.

items.title: No string-literal route paths in Link / navigate
items.rationale: TanStack Router generates a typed route tree. String-literal `to` props defeat type-checking and make refactors unsafe. Training data suggests `<Link to='/plants'>` because react-router-dom works that way.
items.approach: In web-ui/invariants/eslint.config.ts add `no-restricted-syntax` selector: JSXAttribute[name.name='to'] > Literal, scoped to JSX elements with names 'Link', 'Navigate'. Also match useNavigate()('/literal') — CallExpression[callee.name matching naive navigate usage]. Error message: 'Pass `to` as a typed route path from TanStack Router's generated tree, not a literal string.'

acceptance_criteria.description: Add `<Link to='/plants'>...` to any route component. Must fail lint.

items.title: Custom ESLint rule — no vi.mock() on internal modules
items.rationale: Direct TS analog of Python's test_no_patching_production_code. A test that mocks '@/api/...' or '@/features/...' signals the production code has no injection seam — fix production, not the test.
items.approach: Create web-ui/invariants/rules/no-internal-vi-mock.ts — a custom ESLint rule that flags CallExpression where callee is `vi.mock` and first argument is a string literal starting with '@/' or a relative path resolving inside src/. Register via web-ui/invariants/eslint.config.ts as a local rules plugin (use 'eslint-plugin-local' style or the flat-config inline plugin pattern). Tests use MSW (mock Service Worker) at the api-client boundary or inject fakes via QueryClient providers — those seams are legitimate. Append 'local/no-internal-vi-mock' to KNOWN_SENTINELS.

acceptance_criteria.description: Add `vi.mock('@/api/client')` to any .test.ts file. Must fail lint.

items.title: No localStorage / sessionStorage outside shared/storage.ts
items.rationale: Single ownership. Makes tests deterministic (one mock surface) and SSR-safe (one place to shim). Parallel to Python's 'no module-level singletons' principle.
items.approach: In web-ui/invariants/eslint.config.ts: mark web-ui/src/shared/storage.ts as the only file allowed to reference localStorage/sessionStorage. Implement via `no-restricted-globals` scoped by file pattern — use the flat-config `files` array to exempt storage.ts.

acceptance_criteria.description: Add `localStorage.setItem('k','v')` to any component file. Must fail lint.

items.title: No window.* outside shared/platform.ts
items.rationale: Forces testable platform abstraction even in SPA-only apps. Prevents agents from reaching for `window.navigator.clipboard` inline in a component.
items.approach: Same technique as TS-09 (in web-ui/invariants/eslint.config.ts). Mark web-ui/src/shared/platform.ts as the single allowed accessor. Boundary rule or `no-restricted-globals` with per-file override.

acceptance_criteria.description: Add `window.navigator.clipboard.writeText('x')` to any component. Must fail lint.

items.title: No top-level singleton construction outside main.tsx
items.rationale: Direct TS analog of Python's test_no_module_level_singletons. `new QueryClient()` or `createRouter()` at module scope in a feature file creates a hidden singleton — same smell shape, same fix (composition root).
items.approach: In web-ui/invariants/eslint.config.ts use TS-02's boundary element 'main' (src/main.tsx only). Custom rule or no-restricted-syntax: `NewExpression` or `CallExpression[callee.name matching /^(createRouter|createBrowserRouter|createQueryClient|createClient)$/]` at Program scope (not inside a function). Allow only in main.tsx.

acceptance_criteria.description: Add `const qc = new QueryClient()` at module scope in any feature file. Must fail lint.

items.title: knip — unused exports, files, dependencies
items.rationale: Direct TS analog of deptry (PY-02). Agents leave behind dead exports and stray deps after every refactor; knip catches both in one pass.
items.approach: Install knip as dev dep. Fill in web-ui/invariants/knip.json (scaffolded in XX-02) with entry points (src/main.tsx, src/routes/__root.tsx). Add `knip` script to web-ui/package.json that runs `knip --config invariants/knip.json`. Wire into CI via an invariant — add apps/tests/invariants/test_typescript_dead_code.py that shells out to `pnpm --dir web-ui knip --no-progress`. Keeps the failure visible from the Python side too (single source of truth for CI).

acceptance_criteria.description: Add `export const unused = 1;` to any file not re-exported. Must fail knip.

items.title: Generated api-client/ is read-only + drift test
items.rationale: The OpenAPI spec at contracts/webapp-v1.yaml is the FE↔BE contract (per docs/progress/webapp-rewrite.md, Phase 1 freeze). If agents edit generated code, the contract silently drifts. Mirror the Python 'no editing invariant files' hook rule.
items.approach: Two parts. (a) Add a Claude Code hook glob for web-ui/src/api-client/generated/** to .claude/settings.json — same mechanism as XX-02 uses for web-ui/invariants/. (b) Python invariant apps/tests/invariants/test_api_client_not_stale.py regenerates the client into a tmp dir using the codegen tool and `diff -r` against the checked-in directory. Any non-empty diff fails with message 'Run pnpm --dir web-ui run gen:api — do not hand-edit generated code.'

acceptance_criteria.description: Append `// drift` to any file under web-ui/src/api-client/generated/. Drift test must fail.

items.verifier_notes: Depends on contracts/webapp-v1.yaml existing — coordinate with Phase 1 freeze in docs/progress/webapp-rewrite.md. If contract freeze hasn't shipped, mark this item blocked_by=WEBAPP-REWRITE-phase-1-freeze and defer.

items.title: TanStack Router ESLint plugin recommended config
items.rationale: First-party lint rules from the framework — create-route-property-order and friends. Free coverage; no reason not to enable.
items.approach: Install @tanstack/eslint-plugin-router. In web-ui/invariants/eslint.config.ts extend `pluginRouter.configs['flat/recommended']`. If `@typescript-eslint/only-throw-error` is enabled, configure it to allow redirect/notFound as throwable objects (per TanStack docs).

items.title: Tailwind v4 palette guard — ban arbitrary color / size values
items.rationale: Per docs/references/tailwind-v4/INDEX.md the project has a defined palette (paper/ink/magenta + Fraunces/IBM Plex Mono/Inter). Arbitrary values like `bg-[#abc]` or `text-[14px]` sidestep the palette and make visual drift invisible at review time.
items.approach: Custom ESLint rule at web-ui/invariants/rules/no-arbitrary-tw-value.ts OR `eslint-plugin-tailwindcss` v4-compat (check availability at implementation time — v4 support is still landing). Target JSX `className` and template literal contents; flag regex `/\b(?:bg|text|border|ring|fill|stroke)-\[#/` and `/\b(?:w|h|p[xy]?|m[xy]?|text|leading|tracking)-\[\d/`. Allow a short palette-escape list in the rule config for justified arbitrary values. Register in web-ui/invariants/eslint.config.ts.

acceptance_criteria.description: Add `<div className='bg-[#abcdef]'>` anywhere. Must fail lint.

items.title: No inline style={{...}} / <style> tags
items.rationale: Utility-first discipline (Tailwind v4). Inline styles break the palette guard and are un-themeable.
items.approach: In web-ui/invariants/eslint.config.ts add `no-restricted-syntax` selectors: JSXAttribute[name.name='style'], JSXElement[openingElement.name.name='style']. Escape hatch only for dynamically-computed transform/translate values in a short allowlist file (animated reveals etc.).

acceptance_criteria.description: Add `<div style={{color: 'red'}}>` anywhere. Must fail lint.

items.title: Uniform failure-message template for TypeScript lint rules
items.rationale: Python invariants emit SMELL / WHY / FIX / violations via format_invariant_failure — agents learn from that shape fast. The TS custom rules should match so context switching between Python and TS test output is frictionless.
items.approach: Add web-ui/invariants/rules/_message.ts with a template function taking {smell, why, fix, violations[]} and returning the formatted message string. Every custom rule in web-ui/invariants/rules/ uses it. Standard ESLint messages (no-restricted-imports, etc.) get `message` fields filled in the config to loosely match the shape — one line 'WHY: ...' + one line 'FIX: ...'.

open_questions.q: eslint-plugin-boundaries vs dependency-cruiser vs Sheriff — pick one at TS-02 implementation time.
open_questions.bias: eslint-plugin-boundaries — already runs inside ESLint (one tool, one config), Sheriff is newer/less battle-tested, dependency-cruiser is heavier but gives a graph. Pick the plugin unless graph visualization is explicitly wanted.

open_questions.q: Biome vs ESLint for the non-boundary rules (per web-ui pack choosing Biome).
open_questions.bias: Biome for format + fast structural lint; ESLint ONLY where Biome doesn't have an equivalent (custom rules, eslint-plugin-boundaries, TanStack plugin, no-restricted-syntax selectors). Two tools is acceptable here — each has a clear domain. Both configs live inside web-ui/invariants/ — add a biome.json there too if Biome ends up carrying architectural rules.

open_questions.q: Where does contracts/webapp-v1.yaml live and what codegen tool generates the client?
open_questions.bias: Blocked_by the Phase 1 freeze in docs/progress/webapp-rewrite.md. TS-13 defers until that lands. Likely openapi-typescript or hey-api/openapi-ts; pick at freeze time.

open_questions.q: How does the meta-invariant (test_webui_invariants_wired.py) stay in sync with KNOWN_SENTINELS as new TS-* items land?
open_questions.bias: Each TS-* item's approach explicitly says 'append to KNOWN_SENTINELS'. The meta-invariant is protected, so only a human applying a PR review can edit it — agents can't silently drop a sentinel to make their rule 'pass'. If that protection friction becomes painful, reconsider: put KNOWN_SENTINELS in a data file (web-ui/invariants/sentinels.json) that IS protected, and have the test read it. Defer the decision until the first 2-3 TS items have landed and we see how the churn feels.
